  • Changelog

Find Optimal Go/NoGo Thresholds for Two Continuous Endpoints

Source: R/getgamma2cont.R
getgamma2cont.Rd

Computes the optimal Go threshold \(\gamma_1\) and NoGo threshold \(\gamma_2\) for two continuous endpoints by searching over a two-dimensional grid of candidate value pairs. The search follows a two-stage simulate-then-sweep strategy: nsim bivariate datasets are generated from the specified scenario parameters, region probabilities \(\hat{g}_{Go,i}\) and \(\hat{g}_{NoGo,i}\) are computed once for each replicate via pbayespostpred2cont, and operating characteristics under each \((\gamma_1, \gamma_2)\) pair are obtained by a fast proportion-based evaluation without additional simulation.

Usage

getgamma2cont(
  nsim = 10000L,
  prob = "posterior",
  design = "controlled",
  prior = "vague",
  GoRegions,
  NoGoRegions,
  mu1,
  Sigma1,
  mu2 = NULL,
  Sigma2 = NULL,
  target_go,
  target_nogo,
  crit_go = "<",
  crit_nogo = "<",
  sel_go = "smallest",
  sel_nogo = "largest",
  n1,
  n2 = NULL,
  theta.TV1 = NULL,
  theta.MAV1 = NULL,
  theta.TV2 = NULL,
  theta.MAV2 = NULL,
  theta.NULL1 = NULL,
  theta.NULL2 = NULL,
  m1 = NULL,
  m2 = NULL,
  kappa01 = NULL,
  nu01 = NULL,
  mu01 = NULL,
  Lambda01 = NULL,
  kappa02 = NULL,
  nu02 = NULL,
  mu02 = NULL,
  Lambda02 = NULL,
  r = NULL,
  ne1 = NULL,
  ne2 = NULL,
  alpha01e = NULL,
  alpha02e = NULL,
  ybar_e1 = NULL,
  ybar_e2 = NULL,
  Se1 = NULL,
  Se2 = NULL,
  nMC = NULL,
  method = "MC",
  gamma1_grid = seq(0.01, 0.99, by = 0.01),
  gamma2_grid = seq(0.01, 0.99, by = 0.01),
  seed = NULL
)

Arguments

nsim

A positive integer giving the number of Monte Carlo datasets to simulate. Default is 10000L.

prob

A character string specifying the probability type. Must be 'posterior' or 'predictive'.

design

A character string specifying the trial design. Must be 'controlled', 'uncontrolled', or 'external'.

prior

A character string specifying the prior distribution. Must be 'vague' or 'N-Inv-Wishart'.

GoRegions

An integer vector of region indices (subset of 1:9) that constitute the Go region. The 9 regions are defined by the cross-classification of the two treatment effects \((\theta_1, \theta_2)\) relative to (TV1, MAV1) and (TV2, MAV2): region \(k = (z_1 - 1) \times 3 + z_2\) where \(z_i = 1\) if \(\theta_i > TV_i\), \(z_i = 2\) if \(MAV_i < \theta_i \le TV_i\), \(z_i = 3\) if \(\theta_i \le MAV_i\).

NoGoRegions

An integer vector of region indices (subset of 1:9) that constitute the NoGo region. Must be disjoint from GoRegions.

mu1

A length-2 numeric vector giving the true bivariate mean for group 1 (treatment arm) for the scenario used to search for the optimal thresholds.

Sigma1

A 2x2 positive-definite numeric matrix giving the true within-arm covariance in the treatment arm.

mu2

A length-2 numeric vector giving the true bivariate mean for group 2 (control arm). Required for design = 'controlled' or 'external'; set to NULL for design = 'uncontrolled'.

Sigma2

A 2x2 positive-definite numeric matrix giving the true within-arm covariance in the control arm. Required for design = 'controlled' or 'external'; set to NULL for design = 'uncontrolled'.

target_go

A numeric scalar in (0, 1) giving the target value for \(\Pr(\mathrm{Go})\) used to select the optimal \(\gamma_1\). For each candidate \(\gamma_1\), the worst-case \(\Pr(\mathrm{Go})\) over all \(\gamma_2\) in gamma2_grid is compared against target_go using the operator specified by crit_go.

target_nogo

A numeric scalar in (0, 1) giving the target value for \(\Pr(\mathrm{NoGo})\) used to select the optimal \(\gamma_2\). For each candidate \(\gamma_2\), the worst-case \(\Pr(\mathrm{NoGo})\) over all \(\gamma_1\) in gamma1_grid is compared against target_nogo using the operator specified by crit_nogo.

crit_go

A character string specifying the comparison operator applied to \(\Pr(\mathrm{Go})\) when searching for \(\gamma_1\). Must be one of "<", "<=", ">", or ">=". Default is "<".

crit_nogo

A character string specifying the comparison operator applied to \(\Pr(\mathrm{NoGo})\) when searching for \(\gamma_2\). Must be one of "<", "<=", ">", or ">=". Default is "<".

sel_go

A character string specifying whether to select the "smallest" or "largest" value in gamma1_grid satisfying the crit_go criterion. Default is "smallest".

sel_nogo

A character string specifying whether to select the "smallest" or "largest" value in gamma2_grid satisfying the crit_nogo criterion. Default is "largest".

n1

A positive integer giving the number of patients in group 1 (treatment arm) in the PoC trial.

n2

A positive integer giving the number of patients in group 2 (control arm) in the PoC trial. Set to NULL for design = 'uncontrolled'.

theta.TV1

A numeric scalar giving the TV threshold for Endpoint 1. Required when prob = 'posterior'; otherwise set to NULL.

theta.MAV1

A numeric scalar giving the MAV threshold for Endpoint 1. Required when prob = 'posterior'; otherwise set to NULL.

theta.TV2

A numeric scalar giving the TV threshold for Endpoint 2. Required when prob = 'posterior'; otherwise set to NULL.

theta.MAV2

A numeric scalar giving the MAV threshold for Endpoint 2. Required when prob = 'posterior'; otherwise set to NULL.

theta.NULL1

A numeric scalar giving the null hypothesis threshold for Endpoint 1. Required when prob = 'predictive'; otherwise set to NULL.

theta.NULL2

A numeric scalar giving the null hypothesis threshold for Endpoint 2. Required when prob = 'predictive'; otherwise set to NULL.

m1

A positive integer giving the future sample size for group 1. Required when prob = 'predictive'; set to NULL otherwise.

m2

A positive integer giving the future sample size for group 2. Required when prob = 'predictive'; set to NULL otherwise.

kappa01

Positive numeric scalar. NIW prior hyperparameter \(\kappa_{01}\) for group 1. Required when prior = 'N-Inv-Wishart'; otherwise NULL.

nu01

Positive numeric scalar. NIW prior degrees of freedom \(\nu_{01}\) for group 1. Required when prior = 'N-Inv-Wishart'; otherwise NULL.

mu01

Length-2 numeric vector. NIW prior mean \(\mu_{01}\) for group 1. Required when prior = 'N-Inv-Wishart'; otherwise NULL.

Lambda01

A 2x2 positive-definite numeric matrix. NIW prior scale matrix \(\Lambda_{01}\) for group 1. Required when prior = 'N-Inv-Wishart'; otherwise NULL.

kappa02

Positive numeric scalar; see kappa01. For group 2.

nu02

Positive numeric scalar; see nu01. For group 2.

mu02

Length-2 numeric vector; see mu01. For group 2. May be required for the vague prior uncontrolled design; see pbayesdecisionprob2cont.

Lambda02

A 2x2 matrix; see Lambda01. For group 2.

r

A positive numeric scalar giving the power prior weight for group 2 when design = 'uncontrolled' and prior = 'vague'. Otherwise NULL.

ne1

A positive integer giving the external treatment sample size. Required when design = 'external' and external treatment data are used; otherwise NULL.

ne2

A positive integer giving the external control sample size. Required when design = 'external' and external control data are used; otherwise NULL.

alpha01e

A numeric scalar in (0, 1] giving the power prior weight for external treatment data. Required when external treatment data are used; otherwise NULL.

alpha02e

A numeric scalar in (0, 1] giving the power prior weight for external control data. Required when external control data are used; otherwise NULL.

ybar_e1

A length-2 numeric vector. External treatment sample mean. Required when external treatment data are used; otherwise NULL.

ybar_e2

A length-2 numeric vector. External control sample mean. Required when external control data are used; otherwise NULL.

Se1

A 2x2 numeric matrix. External treatment sum-of-squares matrix. Required when external treatment data are used; otherwise NULL.

Se2

A 2x2 numeric matrix. External control sum-of-squares matrix. Required when external control data are used; otherwise NULL.

nMC

A positive integer giving the number of Monte Carlo draws passed to pbayespostpred2cont when method = 'MC'. Set to NULL when method = 'MM'. Default is NULL.

method

A character string specifying the computation method passed to pbayespostpred2cont. Must be 'MC' (default) or 'MM'.

gamma1_grid

A numeric vector of candidate Go threshold values in (0, 1) to search over. Defaults to seq(0.01, 0.99, by = 0.01).

gamma2_grid

A numeric vector of candidate NoGo threshold values in (0, 1) to search over. Defaults to seq(0.01, 0.99, by = 0.01).

seed

An integer value for reproducible random number generation. Default is NULL (no seed set).

Value

A list of class getgamma2cont with the following elements:

gamma1

Optimal Go threshold selected from gamma1_grid according to crit_go and sel_go. NA if no value satisfies the criterion.

gamma2

Optimal NoGo threshold selected from gamma2_grid according to crit_nogo and sel_nogo. NA if no value satisfies the criterion.

PrGo_at_gamma

Pr(Go) evaluated at (gamma1, gamma2). NA if either threshold is NA.

PrNoGo_at_gamma

Pr(NoGo) evaluated at (gamma1, gamma2). NA if either threshold is NA.

gamma1_grid

The candidate Go threshold grid used.

gamma2_grid

The candidate NoGo threshold grid used.

PrGo_grid

Numeric matrix of dimensions length(gamma1_grid) x length(gamma2_grid) giving Pr(Go) at each grid point.

PrNoGo_grid

Numeric matrix of the same dimensions giving Pr(NoGo) at each grid point.

Details

The function uses a two-stage simulate-then-sweep strategy:

Stage 1 (simulation and precomputation): nsim bivariate datasets are generated by drawing from \(N_2(\mu_1, \Sigma_1)\) (and \(N_2(\mu_2, \Sigma_2)\) for controlled/external designs). Sufficient statistics \((\bar{y}_{1,i}, S_{1,i})\) are computed for each replicate, and pbayespostpred2cont is called once in vectorised mode to return an \(nsim \times 9\) matrix of region probabilities. The probabilities are summed over GoRegions and NoGoRegions to obtain \(\hat{g}_{Go,i}\) and \(\hat{g}_{NoGo,i}\) for each replicate, independent of the decision thresholds \((\gamma_1, \gamma_2)\).

Stage 2 (gamma sweep): For each pair \((\gamma_1, \gamma_2)\) in the two-dimensional grid, the operating characteristics are computed as: $$\Pr(\mathrm{Go}) = \frac{1}{n_{sim}} \sum_{i=1}^{n_{sim}} \mathbf{1}\!\left[\hat{g}_{Go,i} \ge \gamma_1,\; \hat{g}_{NoGo,i} < \gamma_2\right]$$ $$\Pr(\mathrm{NoGo}) = \frac{1}{n_{sim}} \sum_{i=1}^{n_{sim}} \mathbf{1}\!\left[\hat{g}_{NoGo,i} \ge \gamma_2,\; \hat{g}_{Go,i} < \gamma_1\right]$$ No additional Monte Carlo draws are required for this step.

Stage 3 (optimal threshold selection): For each candidate \(\gamma_1\), the maximum \(\Pr(\mathrm{Go})\) over all \(\gamma_2\) in gamma2_grid is computed; the optimal \(\gamma_1\) is then chosen as the sel_go value satisfying the crit_go comparison against target_go. Analogously, the optimal \(\gamma_2\) is the sel_nogo value for which the maximum \(\Pr(\mathrm{NoGo})\) over all \(\gamma_1\) satisfies crit_nogo against target_nogo.

Examples

# Example 1: Controlled design, posterior probability, vague prior
# Scenario: mu1 - mu2 = c(5, 1) at MAV boundary (intermediate effect)
# gamma1: smallest gamma1 s.t. max_{gamma2} Pr(Go)   < 0.05
# gamma2: largest  gamma2 s.t. max_{gamma1} Pr(NoGo) < 0.20
if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
Sigma <- matrix(c(6400.0, 15.0, 15.0, 36.0), 2, 2)
getgamma2cont(
  nsim = 1000L, prob = 'posterior', design = 'controlled',
  prior = 'vague',
  GoRegions = 1L, NoGoRegions = 9L,
  mu1 = c(-5.0, 0.0), Sigma1 = Sigma,
  mu2 = c(-10.0, -1.0), Sigma2 = Sigma,
  target_go = 0.05, target_nogo = 0.20,
  crit_go = '<', crit_nogo = '<',
  sel_go = 'smallest', sel_nogo = 'largest',
  n1 = 30L, n2 = 30L,
  theta.TV1 = 10.0, theta.MAV1 = 5.0,
  theta.TV2 = 2.0,  theta.MAV2 = 1.0,
  theta.NULL1 = NULL, theta.NULL2 = NULL,
  m1 = NULL, m2 = NULL,
  kappa01 = NULL, nu01 = NULL, mu01 = NULL, Lambda01 = NULL,
  kappa02 = NULL, nu02 = NULL, mu02 = NULL, Lambda02 = NULL,
  r = NULL,
  ne1 = NULL, ne2 = NULL, alpha01e = NULL, alpha02e = NULL,
  ybar_e1 = NULL, ybar_e2 = NULL, Se1 = NULL, Se2 = NULL,
  nMC = 500L, method = 'MC',
  gamma1_grid = seq(0.01, 0.99, by = 0.01),
  gamma2_grid = seq(0.01, 0.99, by = 0.01),
  seed = 1L
)
} # }

# Example 2: Controlled design, posterior probability, N-Inv-Wishart prior
if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
Sigma  <- matrix(c(6400.0, 15.0, 15.0, 36.0), 2, 2)
Lambda <- matrix(c(6400.0, 15.0, 15.0, 36.0), 2, 2)
getgamma2cont(
  nsim = 1000L, prob = 'posterior', design = 'controlled',
  prior = 'N-Inv-Wishart',
  GoRegions = 1L, NoGoRegions = 9L,
  mu1 = c(-5.0, 0.0), Sigma1 = Sigma,
  mu2 = c(-10.0, -1.0), Sigma2 = Sigma,
  target_go = 0.05, target_nogo = 0.20,
  crit_go = '<', crit_nogo = '<',
  sel_go = 'smallest', sel_nogo = 'largest',
  n1 = 30L, n2 = 30L,
  theta.TV1 = 10.0, theta.MAV1 = 5.0,
  theta.TV2 = 2.0,  theta.MAV2 = 1.0,
  theta.NULL1 = NULL, theta.NULL2 = NULL,
  m1 = NULL, m2 = NULL,
  kappa01 = 0.1, nu01 = 4.0, mu01 = c(0.0, 1.0),  Lambda01 = Lambda,
  kappa02 = 0.1, nu02 = 4.0, mu02 = c(-10.0, -1.0), Lambda02 = Lambda,
  r = NULL,
  ne1 = NULL, ne2 = NULL, alpha01e = NULL, alpha02e = NULL,
  ybar_e1 = NULL, ybar_e2 = NULL, Se1 = NULL, Se2 = NULL,
  nMC = 500L, method = 'MC',
  gamma1_grid = seq(0.01, 0.99, by = 0.01),
  gamma2_grid = seq(0.01, 0.99, by = 0.01),
  seed = 1L
)
} # }

# Example 3: Uncontrolled design, posterior probability, vague prior
if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
Sigma <- matrix(c(6400.0, 15.0, 15.0, 36.0), 2, 2)
getgamma2cont(
  nsim = 1000L, prob = 'posterior', design = 'uncontrolled',
  prior = 'vague',
  GoRegions = 1L, NoGoRegions = 9L,
  mu1 = c(-5.0, 0.0), Sigma1 = Sigma,
  mu2 = NULL, Sigma2 = NULL,
  target_go = 0.05, target_nogo = 0.20,
  crit_go = '<', crit_nogo = '<',
  sel_go = 'smallest', sel_nogo = 'largest',
  n1 = 30L, n2 = NULL,
  theta.TV1 = 10.0, theta.MAV1 = 5.0,
  theta.TV2 = 2.0,  theta.MAV2 = 1.0,
  theta.NULL1 = NULL, theta.NULL2 = NULL,
  m1 = NULL, m2 = NULL,
  kappa01 = NULL, nu01 = NULL, mu01 = NULL, Lambda01 = NULL,
  kappa02 = NULL, nu02 = NULL, mu02 = c(-10.0, -1.0), Lambda02 = NULL,
  r = 1.0,
  ne1 = NULL, ne2 = NULL, alpha01e = NULL, alpha02e = NULL,
  ybar_e1 = NULL, ybar_e2 = NULL, Se1 = NULL, Se2 = NULL,
  nMC = NULL, method = 'MM',
  gamma1_grid = seq(0.01, 0.99, by = 0.01),
  gamma2_grid = seq(0.01, 0.99, by = 0.01),
  seed = 1L
)
} # }

# Example 4: Controlled design, predictive probability, vague prior
# Note: prob = 'predictive' returns 4 regions; GoRegions = 1L (both
# endpoints exceed NULL), NoGoRegions = 4L (both endpoints below NULL)
if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
Sigma <- matrix(c(6400.0, 15.0, 15.0, 36.0), 2, 2)
getgamma2cont(
  nsim = 1000L, prob = 'predictive', design = 'controlled',
  prior = 'vague',
  GoRegions = 1L, NoGoRegions = 4L,
  mu1 = c(-5.0, 0.0), Sigma1 = Sigma,
  mu2 = c(-10.0, -1.0), Sigma2 = Sigma,
  target_go = 0.05, target_nogo = 0.20,
  crit_go = '<', crit_nogo = '<',
  sel_go = 'smallest', sel_nogo = 'largest',
  n1 = 30L, n2 = 30L,
  theta.TV1 = NULL, theta.MAV1 = NULL,
  theta.TV2 = NULL, theta.MAV2 = NULL,
  theta.NULL1 = 5.0, theta.NULL2 = 1.0,
  m1 = 100L, m2 = 100L,
  kappa01 = NULL, nu01 = NULL, mu01 = NULL, Lambda01 = NULL,
  kappa02 = NULL, nu02 = NULL, mu02 = NULL, Lambda02 = NULL,
  r = NULL,
  ne1 = NULL, ne2 = NULL, alpha01e = NULL, alpha02e = NULL,
  ybar_e1 = NULL, ybar_e2 = NULL, Se1 = NULL, Se2 = NULL,
  nMC = 500L, method = 'MC',
  gamma1_grid = seq(0.01, 0.99, by = 0.01),
  gamma2_grid = seq(0.01, 0.99, by = 0.01),
  seed = 1L
)
} # }
